name: The Law Offices of Nathan Miller, PLLC

notableCases.name: Assault Causing Bodily Injury (Class A Misdemeanor)
notableCases.outcome: Dismissed after case was set for trial (firm-reported)

notableCases.name: Felony Theft (Theft of Property < $1500 with 2+ priors)
notableCases.outcome: Declined by the District Attorney's Office before grand jury review; case dismissed (firm-reported)

notableCases.name: Juvenile Felony Robbery
notableCases.outcome: Dismissed after further witness investigation convinced prosecutor (firm-reported)

notableCases.name: DWI
notableCases.outcome: Reduced to Obstruction of a Highway, a non-intoxication offense, before jury trial (firm-reported)

notableCases.name: Boating While Intoxicated
notableCases.outcome: Hung jury; no conviction despite blood test 50% over legal limit (firm-reported)

notableCases.name: DWI with BAC .15 or Higher
notableCases.outcome: Reduced charge with time served after two jury trials ended in mistrials (firm-reported)

websiteQuality.rationale: The site is HTTPS, mobile-ready (viewport meta present), with a clear practice-area navigation, a persistent 'Call Us Today! 940-580-4287' header CTA, contact forms on every page, and a Calendly 'Schedule a Consultation' booking widget (calendly.com/nathanmillerlaw). Trust signals are abundant: Avvo 10.0 and Clients Choice badges, National Trial Lawyers Top 100, TCDLA membership, a dedicated Case Results page, and client testimonials. Accessibility and freshness signals are good (skip-to-content link, alt attributes on all 33 homepage images, JSON-LD structured data, 2026 copyright). It falls short of flawless only because page speed could not be lab-measured and the multi-layer mega-menu is somewhat dense.

factors.mobileUsability: Viewport meta tag present; responsive layout with mobile menu and click-to-call header observed in fetched HTML.
factors.pageSpeed: Not directly measured (no lab tooling available). Inference only: ~145 KB homepage HTML with 14 script tags including third-party Calendly widget suggests moderate page weight, likely acceptable but not optimized to the bone.
factors.accessibility: Skip-to-content link present; all 33 homepage images carry alt attributes; semantic heading structure with clear H1.
factors.visualCredibility: Modern design with attorney/staff photography, award badges (Avvo, National Trial Lawyers, TCDLA), and consistent branding.
factors.navigation: Clear multi-level navigation: practice areas, Meet Our Team, Case Results, Resources, Blog, Contact; slightly dense mega-menu.
factors.contentClarity: Plain-language criminal-defense messaging, per-charge practice pages (DWI, drug, theft, assault, sex crimes, weapons), and 'benefits of hiring a former prosecutor' explainer.
factors.callsToAction: Persistent 'Call Us Today! 940-580-4287' header, free-consultation CTAs throughout, 'Make a Payment' link, and Calendly scheduling badge.
factors.contactFormQuality: Short contact forms (name/phone/email/message) embedded on homepage, contact, team, and practice pages, plus detailed DWI Case Consultation and Criminal Case Consultation intake forms.
factors.localPositioning: Strong: 'Denton Criminal Defense Attorneys' H1, Denton County references throughout, 1701 N. Locust St Denton address, and a stated second office in Southlake serving Grapevine/Colleyville/Keller/Westlake/Trophy Club.
factors.trustSignals: Case Results page with outcomes, client testimonials, Avvo 10.0 rating (2012-2018 per bio), National Trial Lawyers Top 100, Fort Worth Texas Magazine 'Top Attorney', TCDLA board membership, Super Lawyers 2025 selection (per Super Lawyers profile).
factors.appointmentBooking: Calendly badge widget ('Schedule a Consultation', calendly.com/nathanmillerlaw) embedded sitewide, plus detailed online intake forms.

Second office in Southlake, TX serving Grapevine, Colleyville, Keller, Westlake, and Trophy Club.
